scope: Create src/hooks/tool-execution-handler.ts for tool invocation tracking
changes_made: Created new handler file following handleCompaction pattern from compaction-handler.ts
Resolves session via findSessionBySdkId → direct path fallback → initSession
Adds tool_invocation event with toolName and callID at top level
Increments toolCallCount counter with resilient try/catch for partial sessions

npm_test_boundary.result: pre-existing failure: all hook handlers (compaction, chat-message, text-complete, event-handler) already flag mkdir violation

deviations.reason: Boundary check flags mkdir in all hook files
deviations.detail: check-hooks-readonly.sh rejects mkdir in src/hooks/. All existing handlers (compaction-handler.ts:92, chat-message-handler.ts:46, text-complete-handler.ts:170, event-handler.ts:200) have the same violation. This is a pre-existing architectural debt.
deviations.justification: Handler follows the exact same pattern as handleCompaction from compaction-handler.ts which was the reference pattern specified in the delegation packet.

deviations.reason: Event fields (toolName, callID) placed at top level instead of nested in data
deviations.detail: The test asserts toolEvents[0].toolName and toolEvents[0].callID at the event root level, not inside event.data. Used 'as any' cast to satisfy both test expectations and existing AddEventInput interface.
deviations.justification: Test is the contract. The event shape matches what the test expects.

deviations.detail: Counter increment may fail on partial session files missing counters structure. Wrapped in try/catch for resilience.
deviations.justification: Defense against external/partial session files. Error is logged to stderr.

open_issues: Boundary check (check-hooks-readonly.sh) fails for mkdir in ALL hook handlers — needs architectural resolution across the codebase
The 'as any' cast on the event object could be eliminated by extending AddEventInput.event type to support custom fields
